From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

18th United States national census

| Eighteenth census of the United States | |
| --- | --- |
| ---   ← [1950](/wiki/1950_United_States_census "1950 United States census")  **April 1, 1960**  [1970](/wiki/1970_United_States_census "1970 United States census") →   --- | |
| U.S. Census Bureau seal | |
| 1960 U.S. census logo | |
| General information | |
| Country | United States |
| Results | |
| Total population | 179,323,175 (Increase 19%) |
| Most populous state | [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") 16,782,304 |
| Least populous state | [Alaska](/wiki/Alaska "Alaska") 226,167 |

The **1960 United States census**, conducted by the [Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), determined the resident population of the United States to be 179,323,175, an increase of 19 percent over the 151,325,798 persons [enumerated](/wiki/Enumeration "Enumeration") during the [1950 census](/wiki/U.S._census,_1950 "U.S. census, 1950"). This was the first census in which all states recorded a population of over 200,000. This census's data determined the electoral votes for the [1964](/wiki/1964_United_States_presidential_election "1964 United States presidential election") and [1968](/wiki/1968_United_States_presidential_election "1968 United States presidential election") presidential elections. This was also the last census in which [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") was the most populous state.

Detroit lost 220,000 residents in the 1950s, which remains the record for the largest loss of population between two censuses.

## Census questions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_United_States_census&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Census questions")]

The 1960 census collected the following information from all respondents:[[1]](#cite_note-1)

* Address
* Name
* Relationship to Head of Household
* Sex
* Race
* Age
* Marital Status

Approximately 25% of households received a "long form" of the 1960 census, which contained over 100 questions. Full documentation on the 1960 census, including census forms and a procedural history, is available from the [Integrated Public Use Microdata Series](/wiki/Integrated_Public_Use_Microdata_Series "Integrated Public Use Microdata Series").

## Data availability

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_United_States_census&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Data availability")]

[Microdata](/wiki/Microdata_(statistics) "Microdata (statistics)") from the 1960 census are freely available through the [Integrated Public Use Microdata Series](/wiki/Integrated_Public_Use_Microdata_Series "Integrated Public Use Microdata Series"). [Aggregate data](/wiki/Aggregate_data "Aggregate data") for small areas, together with electronic boundary files, can be downloaded from the [National Historical Geographic Information System](/wiki/National_Historical_Geographic_Information_System "National Historical Geographic Information System"). Personally identifiable information will be available in 2032.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
